commit.message:
v10.1.2171: waitlist email — Odlične/Sjajne → Dobre (SL+HR)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix video segments: use I2V from last frame for continuity + full 10s duration

Segments 2+ were using T2V (text-to-video) which produced 5s clips instead
of 10s. Now extracts last frame from completed segment via FFmpeg inline and
starts next segment as I2V (image-to-video), ensuring:
- Visual continuity between segments (same scene, lighting, colors)
- Full 10s duration per segment (Seedance respects duration better with I2V)
- Smooth ending on final segment (gradual slow motion fade)
- Fallback to T2V if frame extraction fails

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Companies: DataTable with sorting on name, contacts, last touch
- Activity Feed: DataTable with type filter, sortable columns
- Pagination: Previous/Next + page numbers with ellipsis pattern
- Page size selector: 10/25/50/100

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: triage summaries include manifest fallback for unprofiled tools (#45)

_build_triage_summaries() only fell back to manifest-derived summaries
when the profiles table was completely empty. With partial profiles
(some tools profiled, others not yet), newly added tools were silently
excluded from triage context, causing the LLM to route to RESPOND.

Now cross-checks DB profiles against the live registry and merges
manifest-derived entries for any on-demand tool missing a profile.

commit.message:
Fix incremental sync skipping new bookmarks due to reverse order

insertTweets reversed the array (oldest first) and stopped on the first
duplicate, which meant new bookmarks at the top were never reached.
Now incremental sync processes newest first without reversing, and
inserts all new bookmarks in the batch before stopping at duplicates.

Add Svenska (sv) localization

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Cut token usage ~90%: throttle TradeSetupGenerator + disable memo Claude calls

TradeSetupGenerator (Sonnet 4.6) was firing every 5 min for BTC + ETH =
~2016 Sonnet calls/week ≈ 4M tokens/week (main culprit for 4.8M weekly spend).

Fix 1: TRADE_SETUP_INTERVAL=3600 — generate trade setups at most once per hour
per asset (was every 5 min). Reduces Sonnet calls from 2016/week to 336/week.

Fix 2: NewsNarrator.generate_market_memo/generate_news_tweet — disabled Claude
path. Memos are file-only artifacts not shown to users; template fallback is
sufficient. Saves ~400K Haiku tokens/week.

Net result: estimated token usage drops from ~4.6M/week to ~450K/week.
Claude budget is now reserved for the 5 intentional daily anchor posts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix proof deletion — use admin client to bypass RLS

The proofs table had no DELETE RLS policy, causing Supabase to silently
ignore deletes (no error returned, 0 rows affected). Created a service
role admin client that bypasses RLS for trusted server-side operations.
The DELETE handler still verifies studio auth before using the admin client.

Also adds migration 003 with explicit DELETE policies as belt-and-suspenders.

commit.message:
fix: handle Pydantic 422 array errors in parseApiError

Pydantic validation errors return detail as an array of objects,
not a string. Passing this array directly to toast.error() caused
a React rendering crash (global error boundary). Now properly
extracts msg from each validation error and joins them.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial release: unified-channel v0.1.0

Unified message middleware for Python with 19 channel adapters:
Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, iMessage, LINE, Matrix,
MS Teams, Feishu, Mattermost, Google Chat, Twitch, IRC, Nostr,
Zalo, BlueBubbles, Nextcloud Talk, Synology Chat.

Features:
- ChannelManager orchestrates adapters + middleware pipeline
- CommandMiddleware for /command routing with decorator API
- AccessMiddleware for sender allowlist
- Custom middleware support (rate limiting, logging, etc.)
- Push messaging + broadcast across channels
- All adapters use lazy imports (install only what you need)
- Zero core dependencies, Python 3.10+

commit.message:
fix: repair migrations 002/003/005 that crash on fresh install

Three migrations assumed tables/columns that schema.sql no longer
creates, causing sqlite3.OperationalError on every fresh install:

• 002_remove_user_id.sql — shadow-copies `moments` to strip user_id,
  but schema.sql no longer creates `moments` (removed by migration 004).
  `INSERT INTO moments_new … SELECT … FROM moments` fails instantly.
  Fix: remove the moments block; migration 004 drops it anyway with
  `DROP TABLE IF EXISTS moments`.

• 003_moments_exchange_index.sql — adds idx_moments_exchange ON moments,
  but moments doesn't exist on a fresh install. Migration 004 drops the
  index too. Fix: replace with a no-op SELECT 1.

• 005_routing_decisions_reasoning.sql — `ALTER TABLE routing_decisions
  ADD COLUMN reasoning TEXT` duplicates the column already declared in
  schema.sql, crashing with "duplicate column name". Fix: replace with a
  no-op SELECT 1 and add the column to the _optional_columns guard in
  database_service.run_pending_migrations() (checks PRAGMA table_info
  before ALTER TABLE — safe for both fresh installs and older databases
  that genuinely lack the column).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix false telecine detection on VHS tapes, bump version to 0.7.1

Scan type detection was misclassifying interlaced PAL VHS content as hard
telecine because any single repeated field (repeatedFields > 0) triggered
telecine. VHS tapes produce stray repeated fields from tracking instability,
especially at the start. Now requires >= 15% repeated fields ratio, matching
real 3:2 pulldown (~40%) while filtering out VHS noise. Also skips the first
2s of longer videos to avoid unstable VHS leader sections.

refactor: apply code review best practices across all modules

- Replace all Serial.printf/println with LOG_INFO/WARN/ERROR macros in
  sensors, ota, provisioning, and display — logs are now compiled out
  in release builds
- Fix double battery I2C read: read voltage once, derive pct via new
  battery_pct_from_mv() — avoids two I2C transactions per wake cycle
- Fix blocking delays in display_show_splash and display_show_cal_done
  by replacing delay() with lv_task_handler() tick loops — LVGL tasks
  stay responsive during timed screens
- Fix delay(5000) in sensor init error path to use display_tick loop
- Replace delay(1) in OTA download loop with display_tick() — keeps
  LVGL progress bar updating during firmware flash
- Remove unused BEEP_API_KEY fallback define from provisioning.cpp

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Happy <yesreply@happy.engineering>

fix(nextcloud): add egress rules to network policy

Adds missing egress rules to allow Nextcloud to:
- Query CoreDNS for DNS resolution (port 53 UDP/TCP)
- Connect to Redis (port 6379 TCP)
- Make external connections (for Collabora callbacks)

Without egress rules, the ingress-only policy implicitly denied all
outbound traffic, breaking DNS resolution and Redis connectivity.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix: bulletproof JSON parser for AI topology responses

Replace all inline JSON parsing with shared extractJSON() and
parseAndMergeTopology() functions that handle every possible format:
- Markdown code fences (```json ... ```) anywhere in text
- Leading/trailing prose text around JSON
- Top-level arrays [ {page1}, {page2} ]
- Top-level objects { pages: [...] }
- Truncated responses (finds matching bracket by nesting depth)
- Proper string-aware bracket matching (ignores brackets inside strings)

Single source of truth used in all 3 parse paths (service + 2 controller endpoints).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: GridVision
repository.description: Gridvision

commit.message:
fix: tool registry race condition and trusted-tool Dockerfile checks (#49, #63, #68, #69, #70, #71)

Three related fixes:
1. Register trusted tools in self.tools BEFORE running pip install so they
   appear immediately in /tools on startup. Dep install was blocking the
   entire registry on fresh containers (30-90s per batch), causing empty
   registry when tests queried before all pip installs finished.
2. Safety-net filesystem scan in list_tools() now accepts runner.py OR
   Dockerfile, so trusted tools appear even if they failed to load into
   the active registry.
3. Install and update endpoints conditionally require Dockerfile (sandboxed)
   or runner.py (trusted) based on the embodiment library trust status,
   allowing trusted tools to be installed without a Dockerfile.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: ラスト15秒パニック演出 + 正解ボーナス+5秒タイム回復を追加

- 残り15秒以下で isPanicMode=true → body.panic クラスで画面シェイク
- body.panic .timer:not(.bonus) → timerPanicPulse で赤点滅ループ
- BGM加速: <20s→280ms下限, <15s→180ms下限でさらに加速
- 正解時(残り≤15s)に bonusMs +5000ms を Firebase 同期 → 両プレイヤーのタイマー即時反映
- 回復演出: .timer.bonus アニメーション(緑グロー)+ "+5 SEC!" フロートテキスト
- 回復音: 880Hz→1320Hz の2音上昇(Web Audio API)
- ボーナス後に残り>15sになればパニックモード自動解除（再突入も対応）
- phase1(PIN正解)/phase2(ワイヤー正解)/phase3(シンボル正解)すべてに対応
- enterFinalArm/enterReview で exitPanicMode() を呼び確実にクリーンアップ

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
